task_completion.description: The EnterpriseServer project is imported into Eclipse.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Eclipse IDE is open and 'EnterpriseServer' is visible in the Package Explorer or Project Explorer view.

task_completion.description: The Eclipse Debugger is attached and execution is paused.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Eclipse Debug perspective is active, and the execution thread is shown as suspended (paused) at a breakpoint, likely within LicenseManager.java.

task_completion.description: The license check variable is modified at runtime using the debugger.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Variables' or 'Expressions' view in the Eclipse Debug perspective shows the 'isLicensed' variable (or similar license-related boolean) being manually changed to 'true' or having its value overridden.

task_completion.description: The ServerBootstrapper application resumes and finishes starting up without license errors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Eclipse Console view shows output indicating the server has successfully started or bootstrapped, without terminating due to an unreachable license server.

task_completion.description: The startup_success.log file is created and contains the success token.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file 'startup_success.log' is visible in the project root in Eclipse, or a terminal/editor shows its contents including the text 'SUCCESS_TOKEN_BYPASS_COMPLETE'.

integrity.description: The Java source files were not modified to bypass the check.
integrity.visual_evidence: The source code for LicenseManager.java in the Eclipse editor still shows the original license check logic (e.g., 'isLicensed = false'). There should be no asterisk (*) on the file tab indicating unsaved changes, and no hardcoded 'true' in the source text.

integrity.description: The bypass was performed using the Eclipse Debugger GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots must show interaction with the Eclipse Debug perspective (Variables, Breakpoints, Debug toolbar) rather than using terminal-based debuggers like 'jdb' or writing a separate Java reflection script.

integrity.description: The success log was generated by the application, not manually created.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal commands like 'echo SUCCESS_TOKEN_BYPASS_COMPLETE > startup_success.log' or manual text editor creation of the log file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project is located at /home/ga/Downloads/EnterpriseServer. The protected file is src/main/java/com/enterprise/server/LicenseManager.java, which contains a boolean 'isLicensed' initialized to false. The agent must use the Eclipse Debugger to change this variable to true at runtime. A successful bypass allows ServerBootstrapper to write 'startup_success.log' to the project root containing the exact token 'SUCCESS_TOKEN_BYPASS_COMPLETE'.
